MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON JUN-05-2012 IN 206, DEFENDANT MULLINS AND COMPANY'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE AND ALL PRETRIAL DISCOVERY DATES. AFTER READING THE PAPERS AND HEARING ARGUMENT, THE COURT DENIED DEFENDANT MULLINS AND CO.'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE. THERE IS NO GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE PARTICULARLY IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT THE NEW CAUSES OF ACTION WHICH APPEAR TO NECESSITATE THE CONTINUANCE IN THE MIND OF DEFENSE COUNSEL ARE BEING DISMISSED BY THE PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT HE WILL BE FILING A REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL ON THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TRICIA ARVEDI AND MULLINS AND COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE, IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO FILE A FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGING FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT AN AWARD OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE PRIMARY ALLEGATIONS IN THE EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ATTACHMENT ARE ALLEGED IN TERMS OF "DEFENDANTS KNEW OR SHOULD HAVE KNOWN'." THIS IS INSUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A SHOWING OF CONSCIOUS DISREGARD. Notice Of Motion To Compel Completion Of Dr Fogelberg'S Deposition And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-02-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF LETICIA CORAL'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLETION OF DR FOGELBERG'S DEPOSITION AND FOR SANCTIONS.
Appearances: Frances Burns of Bledsoe Cathcart Diestel Pederson & Treppa LLP (981.5411) for defendant Mullins & Co.; Philip M. Andersen and unidentified counsel (925.225.6838) for defendant Arvedi; Sandy Cipinko (693.9905) for plaintiff and moving party.
Discussed and the court ruled as follows: the court allows 15 minutes of deposition by telephone. Sanctions and fees are not awarded. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT TRICIA ARVEDI AND MULLINS AND COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted with ten days leave, if plaintiff can do so in good faith, to file a fourth amended complaint alleging facts sufficient to support an award of punitive damages. The primary allegations in the exemplary damages attachment are alleged in terms of "Defendants knew or should have known'." This is insufficient to support a showing of conscious disregard. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
